title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Nikoleteis confronts Mrs. Haberdash, accusing her of betraying secrets to a friend and failing to manage the students' disruptive behavior. Mrs. Nikoleteis demands that a student who admitted to various wrongdoings be expelled, lamenting the ruined reputation of the student home. Mrs. Haberdash retreats to the kitchen, where Maria, appearing tense and uncooperative, refuses to make Italian seasoning and instead offers to make a pale, English-style stew.

That evening, Mrs. Haberdash attempts to restore peace by gathering the students. During dinner, Len Bateson assists Celia by escorting her into the room. The atmosphere shifts when Mr. Akiyama makes a comment about Silvia's skill at stealing, causing Sally to feel suffocated. Amidst the social tension, Colin McNaughton announces his intention to marry Silvia after his studies. While some students offer congratulations, others engage in a debate regarding marriage and age. Celia expresses her belief that the recent incident involving ink on a paper and a backpack will be resolved by the following day.

The following morning, Miss Lemon arrives late to meet Hercule Poirot, breathless and flustered. She informs him that a student named Celia has committed suicide, likely via a morphine overdose, leaving a note behind. Poirot, noting he had anticipated something might happen, instructs Miss Lemon to file his letters while he heads to Chestnut Street. Upon arriving, Jerome recognizes Poirot from the previous evening and conspiratorially informs him that Celia died in her bed. Jerome adds that she had been engaged to Mr. Collins, a tall man who smokes a pipe, just the night before. Jerome offers to notify the mistress once the police have finished their investigation.
